title: Pathways in Biomedical Engineering
description: Overview of biomedical engineering and the biomedical engineering industry, including specialties, degree requirements and scholastic programs in the Department of Biomedical Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Professional Development Essentials
description: Examination of professional development essentials required to be a professional biomedical engineer; topics include academic, engineering, and medical ethics; critical thinking and problem-solving skills; and written, verbal, and mathematical communication skills.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MATH 152 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Computing for Biomedical Engineering
description: Introduction to the principles of computer programming for biomedical applications including program design and development, programming techniques and documentation; introduction to and programming in the Python and LabVIEW environments, and computer-aided design application in SolidWorks environments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MATH 152 and ENGR 102 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomedical Applications of Signals and Systems
description: Quantitative analysis of biomedical and physiological signals; Fourier and Laplace transforms; filtering of biomedical signals; electrical circuits an analog representations of physiological systems as model systems; A/D conversion and sampling.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Admitted to major degree sequence; PHYS 207 or PHYS 208; MATH 308 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Biostatistics and Data Visualization
description: Introduction to statistical thinking for biomedical engineering problem-solving and decision-making applied to the benchtop, laboratory, and clinical testing and to the quality engineering procedures of verification and validation; topics include descriptive statistics, estimation, hypothesis testing, regression and correlation, experimental design, data visualization, and ethics of statistical practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MATH 152 and ENGR 102 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Discovering Biomedical Engineering Design Thinking
description: Identification and exploration of what constitutes a medical device and the unique aspects of medical device design that are imposed by medical device regulation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomedical Engineering Design I
description: Introduction into biomedical engineering design and relevant biomedical topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 253 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Directed Studies
description: Allows students to undertake special projects in biomedical engineering at an earlier point in their studies than required for BMEN 485 .
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Approval of Director of Undergraduate Programs.

title: Special Topics in...
description: Selected topics in an identified area of biomedical engineering. May be repeated for credit.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Approval of instructor.

title: Research
description: Research conducted under the direction of faculty member in biomedical engineering. May be repeated 2 times for credit.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Freshman or sophomore classification and approval of instructor.
repeatability: May be repeated 2 times for credit.

title: Bioinstrumentation
description: Introduction to biomedical instrumentation design; hands on acquisition of biomedical signals; design, building and testing of bioinstrumentation circuits including analog signal amplifiers and analog filter circuits.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 211 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 321 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Imaging Living Systems
description: Examination of microscopy and clinical imaging modalities in biomedical applications across intracellular to whole body length scales; microscopy topics include optical, electron, scanning probe, infrared, intravital, and other techniques; clinical imaging techniques to be covered include x-ray, ultrasound, magnetic resonance imaging, computed and positron emission tomography, and other specialized clinical imaging.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PHYS 207 and BMEN 207 or approval of instructor; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Signals, Power, Acquisition, tRansforms and Knowledge (SPARK) Laboratory
description: Exposure to foundational biomedical instrumentation design; hands-on acquisition of biomedical signals; design, building and testing of bioinstrumentation circuits including analog signal amplifiers and analog filter circuits.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 321 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 250 or STAT 312 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Circuits, Signals and Systems
description: Examination of circuits and linear and nonlinear systems concepts required for quantitative analysis of biomedical and physiological signals and design of biomedical systems; topics include electrical circuit fundamentals, operational amplifiers, frequency response, electrical transients, impulse response, transfer functions, convolution, Fourier and Laplace transforms, filtering of biomedical signals, electrical circuits and analog representations of physiological systems as model systems and A/D conversion and sampling; theoretical investigations and hands-on acquisition of biomedical signals and designing, building.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 320 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 207 , BMEN 250 or STAT 312 , MATH 308 and PHYS 207 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biosignal Analysis
description: Design and application of analog and digital signal analysis in biomedical engineering; characteristics of biomedical signals; design considerations for analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog circuitry; biosignal transformation methods; analog and digital filter design for biomedical signals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 321 .

title: Biotransport
description: Fundamentals of momentum, mass, and energy transport related to living or biomedical systems; examination of the basic principles and constitutive equations of a variety of biological transport phenomena, with length scales ranging from intracellular to organ level; topics include fluid mechanics; transport by diffusion, along with effects of convection, electrochemical potential, and chemical reactions; and energy-tissue interactions; emphases are given to fundamental principles, quantitative approaches, and biomedical applications of these principles and techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 207 or concurrent enrollment; grade of C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 ; grade of C or better in MATH 308 ; grade of C or better in PHYS 207 or PHYS 208; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomaterials Characterization and Testing Laboratory
description: Principles and techniques used in the preparation and characterization of biomaterials, including metals, ceramics, polymers and composites; microstructural, chemical/compositional and surface analysis methods; hands-on acquisition, interpretation and analysis of the characterization results.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 343 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 250 or STAT 312 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomedical Engineering Materials
description: Properties, preparation and characterization of natural and man-made materials encountered in biomedical applications; topics include the chemical structure of metals, ceramics and polymers; physical, mechanical, bulk and surface properties of biomaterials; biomaterial degradation; and biomaterial processing; theoretical investigations and interpretation of biomaterial preparation and characterization data.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 342 ; grade of C or better in MATH 308 and BMEN 361 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biological Interactions and Testing
description: Application of quantitative engineering principles and bioimaging, bioassay, and biomolecule activity testing to elucidate the interactions between materials and biological systems; topics include protein and cell interactions with biomaterials; biomaterial implantation; acute inflammation, wound healing and the presence of biomaterials immune responses to biomaterials; thrombosis; infection; tumorigenesis; and calcification of biomaterials; examination of theoretical investigations and interpretation of biological interaction data.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 345 ; grade of C or better in MATH 308 ; grade of C or better in VTPP 435 or concurrent enrollment; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biological Interactions and Testing Laboratory
description: Hands-on acquisition and interpretation of biological interactions between materials and biological systems; design and use of quantitative assays according to ISO and other professional standards.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 344 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 250 or STAT 312 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomedical and Health Data Science
description: Exploration of applications of data analytics, machine learning, and deep learning in health sciences and biomedical data; topics include theoretical foundations, algorithms and methods of deriving valuable insights from data, predictive health analysis, electronic health records, medical image analysis, computational drug discovery, and genome structure prediction using predictive modeling.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 207 and BMEN 250 or STAT 312 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomedical Engineering Device Design II
description: Identification of needs, concept ideation and selection, and creating an initial prototype for user testing within the biomedical engineering process.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 254 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomedical Engineering Design III
description: Analysis of validation and verification testing for biomedical engineering design, along with manufacturing readiness, market-entry readiness, and post-market engineering activities.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 353 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomechanics Laboratory
description: Hands-on acquisition and interpretation of biomechanical data from mechanical testing of biomedical devices, biomaterials and hard and soft biological tissues.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 361 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 250 or STAT 312 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Biomedical Engineering Mechanics
description: Examination of biomedical engineering mechanics with a focus on biomedical devices, biomaterials and hard and soft biological tissues; topics include fundamentals of static mechanics and mechanics of materials supplemented with experimental design, simulation and mechanical testing; exploration of theoretical investigations; interpretation and application of biomechanical data.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Concurrent enrollment in BMEN 360 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 207 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Engineering Professional Development
description: Participation in an approved high-impact learning practice; reflection on professional outcomes from engineering body of knowledge; documentation and self-assessment of learning experience at mid-curriculum point.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Admission to biomedical engineering; junior or senior classification or approval of instructor.

title: History of Human and Veterinary Medicine in Europe
description: Addresses the major developments in human and veterinary medicine in Europe from the Middle Ages to the present; explores key events and figures in medical history and analyzes issues of current biomedical concern in a historical context; for example, animal rights, ethics of humane experimentation, euthanasia.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Admitted to major degree sequence in biomedical engineering; VTPP 434 . Cross Listing: VTPP 401/BMEN 400 .

title: Principles and Analysis of Biological Control Systems
description: Techniques for generating quantitative mathematical models of physiological control systems and devices; the behavior of physiological control systems using both time and frequency domain methods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 321 .

title: Biomedical Optics Laboratory
description: Biomedical optics technology; basic engineering principles used in developing therapeutic and diagnostic devices; hands-on labs including optical monitoring, diagnostic and therapeutic experiments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: PHYS 207 or PHYS 208 or approval of instructor; Biomedical Engineering major or minor.

title: FDA Good Laboratory and Clinical Practices
description: Implementation of Good Laboratory Practices (GLP) for the submission of preclinical studies and use of Good Clinical Practices (GCP) in clinical trials in accordance with Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations; includes similarities and differences in GLP and GCP critical for the introduction of new drugs and medical devices.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: BMEN 253 ; junior or senior classification.

title: Medical Device Path to Market
description: Path to market for a medical device with specific attention to the regulatory affairs to enable the development of an appropriate regulatory strategy due to the highly regulated global environment.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: BMEN 253 ; junior or senior classification, or approval of instructor.

title: Medical Imaging
description: Principles of major imaging modalities including x-ray radiography, x-ray computed tomography (CT), ultrasonography and magnetic resonance imaging; including a brief discussion on other emerging imaging technologies such as nuclear imaging (PET and SPECT).
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 321 ; junior or senior classification.

title: Bioelectromagnetism
description: Electric, magnetic and electromagnetic phenomena associated with biological tissues; source modeling based on physiological current including line and volume conductor models as well as electromagnetic-based stimulation, sensing and imaging.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 321 or approval of instructor.

title: Biophotonics
description: Theory and application of optical instrumentation, including light sources, lasers, detectors, and optical fibers; instrumentation and engineering in biomedical applications of optics in therapeutics, diagnostics, and biosensing.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 311 ; Biomedical Engineering majors; junior or senior classification.

title: Magnetic Resonance Engineering
description: Design, construction and application of instrumentation for MR imaging; fundamentals of the architecture of an MR spectrometer and the gradient subsystem used for image localization; emphasis on the radiofrequency sensors and systems used for signal generation and reception.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ECEN 322 or BMEN 420 ; junior or senior classification.

title: Embedded Systems for Medical Applications
description: Principles of embedded system architecture and programming; fundamentals and theoretical foundations of wireless communication systems; hands-on experiences of how an embedded system could be used to solve problems in biomedical engineering; projects on wireless sensors and imaging for medical devices.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 321 , CSCE 350/ECEN 350 , or CSCE 315 , or approval of instructor. Cross Listing: CSCE 461/BMEN 428 .

title: Advanced Microfabrication in Microfluidics
description: Microfabrication techniques for designing sensors and platforms for point-of-care diagnostics; technical understanding of biosensors and different integrated devices such as implantable, injectable and actuation systems; examination of various microfabrication processes for materials ranging from polymers to glass; topics include microfluidics, lithography, vapor deposition systems, laser micromachining and various characterization techniques; emphasis on processes as well as the application of these processes and techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 321 ; junior or senior classification.

title: Biomolecular Engineering
description: Foundations for understanding and experimental approaches for measuring and manipulating biomolecules; proteins, nucleic acids and carbohydrates; thermodynamics and kinetics of biomolecular reactions.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 344 or concurrent enrollment; junior or senior classification; Biomedical Engineering majors; or approval of instructor.

title: Molecular and Cellular Biomechanics
description: Introduces biomolecules and their assemblies that play structural and dynamical roles in subcellular to cellular level mechanics; emphasis on quantitative/theoretical descriptions; discussions of the relevant experiment approaches to probe these nano to micro-scale phenomena; includes topics in self-assembly of cytoskeleton and biomembranes, molecular motors, cell motility, and mechanotransduction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: BMEN 361 .

title: Biomolecular and Cellular Engineering Laboratory
description: Laboratory biosafety and biohazard awareness; cell culture protocols and standards for biocompatibility testing; setting protocols for cellular and biomolecular projects; bioimaging, bioassays and biomolecule activity testing.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: VTPP 435 ; BMEN 431 or concurrent enrollment; majors in biomedical engineering; junior or senior classification; or approval of instructor.

title: Healthcare Technology in the Developing World
description: Principles of operation for major types of medical equipment; physiology underlying the measurement; major functional (system) pieces for each instrument; typical problems/applications of each instrument.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MATH 152 and PHYS 207 ; approval of instructor.

title: Case Studies
description: Examination of the process through which clinically defined problems are addressed from the perspective of biomedical engineering through the use of case studies; issues of technology transfer and clinical evaluation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admitted to major degree sequence; junior or senior classification.

title: Mass and Energy Transfer in Biosystems
description: Transport phenomena associated with physiological systems and their interaction with medical devices; exchange processes in artificial life support systems and diagnostic equipment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 341 ; Biomedical Engineering major or minor.

title: Analysis and Design Project I
description: Team-based biomedical engineering analysis and design project involving needs statement generation, ideation, technical specifications, subsystem analysis and design of complete biomedical system to fit unique needs.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 321 , BMEN 344 , BMEN 354 , BMEN 361 and VTPP 435 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Analysis and Design Project II
description: Continuation of BMEN 453 .
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 453 ; Biomedical Engineering major.

title: Orthopedic Biomechanics
description: Development of competencies in biomechanical principles using practical examples and clinical case studies; application of biomechanical knowledge to the evaluation of musculoskeletal tissues and structures, and treatment options for musculoskeletal dysfunction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 361 or equivalent course approved by instructor.

title: Motion Biomechanics
description: Skeletal anatomy and mechanics; muscle anatomy and mechanics; theory and application of electromyography; motion and force measuring equipment and techniques; inverse dynamics modeling of the human body; current topics in musculoskeletal biomechanics research.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: BMEN 207 or approval of instructor; junior or senior classification.

title: Cardiac Mechanics
description: In-depth study of the mechanics of the heart and myocardium with focus on hemodynamic metrics such as Wiggers, PV loop, contractility, quantitative anatomy, heart wall motion and modeling fallacies.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: BMEN 341 , or concurrent enrollment; BMEN 361 .

title: Soft Tissue Mechanics and Finite Element Methods
description: Application of continuum mechanics and finite element methods to the study of the mechanical behavior of soft tissues and associative applications in biomedicine.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 341 and BMEN 361 .

title: Cancer Bioengineering
description: Examination of cancer from an engineering perspective focusing on cancer as a complex interaction of tumor cells, support cells, and immune cells; quantifying and engineering tumor microenvironments; engineering advances in cancer discovery and therapeutic interventions; emphasis on systems integration to address cancer challenges.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 343 and VTPP 435 ; grade of C or better in BMEN 480 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Clinical Engineering for Cardiac Rhythm Management and Ablation
description: Principles of devices, technical marketing, and clinician-facing roles in cardiac rhythm management and ablation.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 461 and BMEN 321 ; senior classification or approval of instructor.

title: Entrepreneurial Pathways in Medical Devices
description: Overview of fundamental elements and development steps for an effective strategy pathway including regulatory pathway for commercialization of medical product/medical device innovations; application of the basic regulations and associated requirements and enforcements for product market approval; exploration of product quality test method design requirements; understanding of the applicable regulations and standards pertaining to the design, testing, approval and marketing of medical devices.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Admitted to major degree sequence (upper-level) in biomedical engineering.

title: Numerical Methods in Biomedical Engineering
description: Application of numerical analysis to molecular, cellular and physiological systems using computational techniques to approximate solutions for complex mathematical problems. Topics include finding roots, solving linear systems, interpolation, differentiation, integration and differential equations, emphasizing algorithm efficiency, error analysis and implementation using modern computing software.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 207 and VTPP 434 .

title: Computational Fluid Dynamics in Biomedical Engineering
description: Fundamentals of computational fluid dynamics (CFD) for biomedical applications; topics include discretization of fundamental balance relations; modeling physiologic geometries; mesh generation; modeling physics; iterative solution approach; and analysis of simulation results; emphasis on the role of CFD in biomedical device design and patient-tailored medicine.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in BMEN 341 ; junior or senior classification or approval of instructor.

title: Biomedical Engineering of Tissues
description: Introduction to aspects of tissue engineering with and emphasis placed on tissue level topics including tissue organization and biological processes, with insights from recent literature (state-of-the-art).
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 343 .

title: Seminar
description: Research based seminar focused on the identification and pursuit of research experiences within the local biomedical engineering community. Must be taken on a satisfactory/unsatisfactory basis.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: BMEN major; junior or senior classification.

title: Polymeric Biomaterials
description: Preparation, properties, and biomedical applications of polymers including polymerization; structure-property relationships; molecular weight and measurement; morphology; thermal transitions; network formation; mechanical behavior; polymetric surface modification; polymer biocompatibility and bioadhesion; polymers in medicine, dentistry, and surgery; polymers for drug delivery; polymeric hydrogels; and biodegradable polymers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 343 .

title: Polymeric Biomaterial Synthesis
description: Overview of polymer synthetic routes and key structure-property relationships with emphasis on the design of polymeric systems to achieve specific properties; tissue engineering and drug delivery applications will be used as model systems to explore the process of biomaterial design from synthesis to device evaluation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BMEN 343 .

title: Internship
description: Industry experience from both an experiential perspective as well as networking; reflect upon the experience gained during an internship with an outside entity; learn what it takes to be successful in industry.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Admission to Biomedical Engineering major; BMEN 253 .

title: Directed Studies
description: Allows students to undertake special projects in biomedical engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Approval of instructor or Director of Undergraduate Programs.

title: Biomedical Nanotechnology
description: Nanotechnology applications in biomedicine; concepts of scale; unique properties at the nanoscale; biological interaction, transport, and biocompatibility of nanomaterials; current research and development of nanotechnology for medical applications, including sensors, diagnostic tools, drug delivery systems, therapeutic devices, and interactions of cells and biomolecules with nanostructured surfaces.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: BMEN 343 , senior classification or approval of instructor.

title: Drug Delivery
description: Mechanisms for controlled release of pharmaceutically active agents and the development of useful drug delivery systems; controlled release mechanisms including diffusive, convective, and erosive driving forces by using case studies related to oral, topical and parenteral release in a frontier interdisciplinary scientific research format.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: BMEN 343 ; senior classification in biomedical engineering or approval of instructor.

title: Special Topics in...
description: New or unique areas of biomedical engineering which are of interest to biomedical engineering and other undergraduate students.

title: Research
description: Research conducted under the direction of faculty member in biomedical engineering. May be repeated for credit.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Junior or senior classification and approval of instructor.
